qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded password length enables CPU exhaustion via bcrypt hashing in user signup
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets unbounded password length reaching bcrypt during user signup, while the advisory targets MongoDB operator injection through the token field in password reset and email verification flows.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User enumeration via divergent error messages in verification email endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets distinguishable verification-email error messages for user enumeration; it does not identify the advisory's missing token type validation or MongoDB query-operator injection path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: unauthenticated MongoDB NoSQL operator injection through the token field in password reset or email verification resend endpoints caused by missing endpoint-level token type validation. The qualifying findings instead cover bcrypt password hashing cost on signup and verification-email user enumeration via divergent messages.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-vgjh-hmwf-c588 describes NoSQL injection via the token field in password reset and email verification resend endpoints, enabling extraction of reset or verification tokens.
bcrypt_password_dos/vulnerability_report.json describes POST /users password input reaching bcrypt.hash(password, 10) without a length check.
user_enumeration_verification_email/vulnerability_report.json describes POST /verificationEmailRequest returning different errors for existing versus non-existing email addresses.
Neither qualifying report names the vulnerable token field, password reset token query, email verification token query, MongoDB operator injection, or missing token type validation root cause.
